gbtmkit 0.3.0

Apply GRoLTS acceptance criteria to a shape table

Source: R/grolts-criteria.R
apply_grolts_criteria.Rd

Keeps the candidate shapes whose worst group satisfies all of the GRoLTS adequacy thresholds and orders the survivors by the chosen criterion. The recommended model is the top row.

Usage

apply_grolts_criteria(
  shapes,
  pms_min = 0.05,
  appa_min = 0.7,
  occ_min = 5,
  order_by = "bic"
)

Arguments

shapes

A gbtm_shapes object or its $table data frame (from evaluate_shapes()).

pms_min

Minimum smallest-group assigned proportion (default 0.05).

appa_min

Minimum average posterior probability of assignment (default 0.70).

occ_min

Minimum odds of correct classification (default 5).

order_by

Column to sort survivors by, ascending (default "bic").

Value

A data frame of surviving shapes ordered by order_by, carrying an attribute "recommended" (its first row, or NULL if none qualify) and "thresholds". Class gbtm_criteria.

Examples

# \donttest{
data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit")
spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),
                  paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial")
sh <- evaluate_shapes(spec, n_groups = 4, verbose = FALSE)
apply_grolts_criteria(sh)
# }
